heading: Benefits under certain terminated plans
label: 1441 Benefits under certain terminated plans
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (c)(2)(C), was repealed by , , .Section 1425 of this titlePub. L. 113–235, div. O, title I, § 108(a)(1)128 Stat. 2786Dec. 16, 2014
Effective DatePart effective , except as specifically provided, see .Sept. 26, 1980section 1461(e) of this title
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (c)(2)(C), was repealed by , , .Section 1425 of this titlePub. L. 113–235, div. O, title I, § 108(a)(1)128 Stat. 2786Dec. 16, 2014

children.heading: Amendment of plan by plan sponsor to reduce benefits, and suspension of benefit payments
children.label: a Amendment of plan by plan sponsor to reduce benefits, and suspension of benefit payments
children.text: Notwithstanding sections 1053 and 1054 of this title, the plan sponsor of a terminated multiemployer plan to which applies shall amend the plan to reduce benefits, and shall suspend benefit payments, as required by this section.section 1341a(d) of this title

children.heading: Determinations respecting value of nonforfeitable benefits under terminated plan and value of assets of plan
children.label: b Determinations respecting value of nonforfeitable benefits under terminated plan and value of assets of plan

children.text: The value of nonforfeitable benefits under a terminated plan referred to in subsection (a), and the value of the plan’s assets, shall be determined in writing, in accordance with regulations prescribed by the corporation, as of the end of the plan year during which becomes applicable to the plan, and each plan year thereafter.section 1341a(d) of this title
For purposes of this section, plan assets include outstanding claims for withdrawal liability (within the meaning of ).section 1301(a)(12) of this title

children.heading: Amendment of plan by plan sponsor to reduce benefits for conservation of assets; factors applicable
children.label: c Amendment of plan by plan sponsor to reduce benefits for conservation of assets; factors applicable

children.text: If, according to the determination made under subsection (b), the value of nonforfeitable benefits exceeds the value of the plan’s assets, the plan sponsor shall amend the plan to reduce benefits under the plan to the extent necessary to ensure that the plan’s assets are sufficient, as determined and certified in accordance with regulations prescribed by the corporation, to discharge when due all of the plan’s obligations with respect to nonforfeitable benefits.

children.label: A
children.text: reduce benefits only to the extent necessary to comply with paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: reduce accrued benefits only to the extent that those benefits are not eligible for the corporation’s guarantee under ;section 1322a(b) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: comply with the rules for and limitations on benefit reductions under a plan in reorganization, as prescribed in section 1425 of this title, except to the extent that the corporation prescribes other rules and limitations in regulations under this section; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: D
children.text: take effect no later than 6 months after the end of the plan year for which it is determined that the value of nonforfeitable benefits exceeds the value of the plan’s assets.

children.heading: Suspension of benefit payments; determinative factors; powers and duties of plan sponsor; retroactive benefit payments
children.label: d Suspension of benefit payments; determinative factors; powers and duties of plan sponsor; retroactive benefit payments

children.text: In any case in which benefit payments under a plan which is insolvent under paragraph (2)(A) exceed the resource benefit level, any such payments which are not basic benefits shall be suspended, in accordance with this subsection, to the extent necessary to reduce the sum of such payments and such basic benefits to the greater of the resource benefit level or the level of basic benefits, unless an alternative procedure is prescribed by the corporation in connection with a supplemental guarantee program established under .section 1322a(g)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the plan has been amended to reduce benefits to the extent permitted by subsection (c), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the plan’s available resources are not sufficient to pay benefits under the plan when due for the plan year; and

children.label: B
children.text: “resource benefit level” and “available resources” have the meanings set forth in paragraphs (2) and (3), respectively, of .section 1426(b) of this title

The plan sponsor of a plan which is insolvent (within the meaning of paragraph (2)(A)) shall have the powers and duties of the plan sponsor of a plan in reorganization which is insolvent (within the meaning of ), except that regulations governing the plan sponsor’s exercise of those powers and duties under this section shall be prescribed by the corporation, and the corporation shall prescribe by regulation notice requirements which assure that plan participants and beneficiaries receive adequate notice of benefit suspensions.section 1426(b)(1) of this title

A plan is not required to make retroactive benefit payments with respect to that portion of a benefit which was suspended under this subsection, except that the provisions of section 1426(c)(4) and (5) of this title shall apply in the case of plans which are insolvent under paragraph (2)(A), in connection with the plan year during which such first became applicable to the plan and every year thereafter, in the same manner and to the same extent as such provisions apply to insolvent plans in reorganization under , in connection with insolvency years under such .section 1341a(d) of this titlesection 1426 of this titlesection 1426 of this title
